title: Description of Surya Shakti and Vaishnava Shakti

analysis.core_story_summary: Maitreya says- "Lord, if only the seven Ganas are entrusted with the job of causing cold and summer, drought and rain, what is the purpose then of the Sun’s existence? " Parashar says- It is true that Surya is one of the seven Ganas. Yet being the chief among them, it has special significance.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Mostly suitable for broad audiences with light cultural context.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Description of Surya Shakti and Vaishnava Shakti — Kids (8-12):** Maitreya says- "Lord, if only the seven Ganas are entrusted with the job of causing cold and summer, drought and rain, what is the purpose then of the Sun’s existence? " Parashar says- It is true that Surya is one of the seven Ganas. Yet being the chief among them, it has special significance. This story teaches respect for nature. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
